title: Transvenous embolization of a cerebrospinal fluid–venous fistula for the treatment of spontaneous intracranial hypotension
abstract: Cerebrospinal fluid-venous fistula is an increasingly recognized cause of spontaneous intracranial hypotension. The site of the leak is between the dural sleeve around a spinal nerve root and the surrounding foraminal veins. In appropriately investigated patients, transvenous embolization of the draining foraminal and paraspinal veins has been shown to be an effective way of treating the disease.
out_of_scope_note: Clinical neurointerventional surgery video case report on transvenous embolization of CSF-venous fistula — surgical/interventional clinical scope, not cognitive/perceptual/computational neuroscience.
